description_long: This public toilets structure features a combination of geometric forms, including a large rectangular black block, a segmented light gray enclosure, and two cylindrical units with a textured brown finish. The main enclosure appears to be constructed from tiled or paneled materials, with a partially open roof revealing interior fixtures. The cylindrical sections likely serve as individual stalls or urinals, while the black block may house utility or maintenance areas. The entrance is defined by a lighter-colored door, and the overall footprint is set on a tiled base, suggesting installation in a paved urban environment. The design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on privacy and durability.
description: A modern public toilets facility with a tiled gray enclosure, two cylindrical brown stalls, and a large black utility block, all set on a tiled base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a light-colored door, the segmented gray enclosure, and the two cylindrical brown stalls to the left.
description_view_1: The rear view is dominated by the large black rectangular block, with the gray enclosure and cylindrical stalls partially visible behind it.
description_view_2: This side view highlights the open top of the gray enclosure, the black block on the left, and the two cylindrical stalls on the right.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the two cylindrical brown stalls in the foreground, the light-colored door, and the black block at the back.
attribute_roof_structure: Flat roof with partial open sections, constructed from the same gray panel material as the walls, no visible skylights or overhangs.
